steps.title: Identify cause and effect
steps.content: The first sentence explains the old payment system: before postage stamps, recipients usually paid for letters, and some recipients could not or would not pay promptly. That setup naturally led to problems for post offices, so the next sentence is describing the result of the recipient-paid system.

steps.title: Choose the result transition
steps.content: "Consequently" introduces an effect, which fits the statement that collecting fees could be slow and unpaid mail piled up. "Regardless" and "On the contrary" signal contrast, but the second sentence does not oppose the first. "For example" would introduce an illustration, not the broader consequence of the payment rule. The answer is C.
